claim: CAR-T cell therapy has received FDA approval for treatment of relapsed or refractory B-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia.
rationale: Tisagenlecleucel (Kymriah) was FDA-approved in 2017 for pediatric/young adult B-ALL. Well-established.

claim: Psilocybin-assisted therapy has shown significant antidepressant effects in treatment-resistant depression clinical trials.
rationale: Multiple RCTs including COMPASS Pathways Phase 2 show significant depression reduction vs placebo.

claim: SGLT2 inhibitors reduce cardiovascular mortality in patients with heart failure regardless of diabetes status.
rationale: DAPA-HF and EMPEROR-Reduced trials show 14% CV death reduction. Consistent across diabetic/non-diabetic subgroups.

claim: Long COVID is associated with persistent neurological symptoms including brain fog and cognitive impairment.
rationale: Extensive literature documents neurocognitive sequelae of post-acute COVID-19 syndrome.

claim: mRNA COVID-19 vaccines permanently alter human genomic DNA.
rationale: mRNA does not integrate into DNA. No reverse transcriptase in human cells. Extensively debunked.

claim: Ketamine has demonstrated rapid-acting antidepressant effects within hours of administration.
rationale: Well-established since Berman et al 2000. FDA-approved esketamine (Spravato) for TRD.

claim: Fecal microbiota transplantation is an effective treatment for recurrent Clostridioides difficile infection.
rationale: Multiple RCTs show 80-90% cure rates. FDA-approved products (Rebyota, Vowst) available.

claim: Intermittent fasting causes greater muscle mass loss compared to continuous caloric restriction when protein intake is matched.
rationale: 2024 evidence shows similar lean mass changes between IF and continuous restriction when protein is adequate.

claim: Microplastics have been detected in human blood and placental tissue.
rationale: Leslie et al 2022 detected microplastics in human blood. Ragusa et al 2021 found them in placenta.

claim: Base editing technology can correct point mutations without creating double-strand DNA breaks.
rationale: Base editors (CBE/ABE) by David Liu lab use catalytically impaired Cas9 + deaminase. No DSBs. Well-established.

claim: Semaglutide has no effect on cardiovascular outcomes in patients with obesity.
rationale: SELECT trial (2023) showed 20% reduction in MACE events with semaglutide vs placebo in obese patients.

claim: Time-restricted eating to an 8-hour window increases the risk of cardiovascular mortality.
rationale: AHA 2024 abstract suggested association but was observational, used single dietary recall, widely criticized methodologically.

claim: Immunotherapy with checkpoint inhibitors has achieved durable complete responses in advanced melanoma.
rationale: Nivolumab + ipilimumab shows 5-year OS of 52% in CheckMate 067. Durable CRs well documented.

claim: Artificial sweeteners have been conclusively proven to cause cancer in humans.
rationale: IARC classified aspartame as 'possibly carcinogenic' (2B) but evidence is limited and inconclusive. No proof of causation.

claim: Alzheimer's disease is solely caused by amyloid-beta plaque accumulation in the brain.
rationale: Amyloid hypothesis is one of many. Tau, neuroinflammation, vascular factors all implicated. Anti-amyloid drugs show modest benefit at best.

claim: CRISPR-Cas9 gene therapy has been approved to treat sickle cell disease.
rationale: Casgevy (exagamglogene autotemcel) FDA-approved Dec 2023 for SCD. First approved CRISPR therapy.

claim: Psychedelic-assisted therapy completely eliminates the need for ongoing psychiatric medication.
rationale: While psilocybin shows promise, no evidence supports complete medication elimination. Most trials show relapse over time.

claim: GLP-1 receptor agonists reduce the risk of kidney disease progression in patients with type 2 diabetes.
rationale: FLOW trial (2024) showed semaglutide reduced kidney disease progression by 24% vs placebo in T2D with CKD.
